title: Default Invited User to Last Accessed Team on Login
story: As a DataTools Pro user invited into another team, I want to default to the invited team on login rather than my personal team, so that I immediately see the context I most recently worked in.
description:
## Behavior
On login, the system checks the user's last accessed team and defaults to it. Fallback logic:
1. Load the last team the user accessed.
2. If that team no longer exists, OR the user no longer belongs to it, OR they are not authorized — fall back to the user's personal instance.

This persists the user's team context across login sessions.
acceptance_criteria: User defaults to their last accessed team on login
If last team is invalid/inaccessible, user falls back to personal instance
Team context is persisted across login sessions
Switching teams updates the persisted last-accessed team
ux_changes: Users default to their last accessed team on login instead of always landing on their personal instance.
middleware_changes: Login flow checks last accessed team and validates membership/authorization before defaulting.
data_model_changes: New field to track the user's last accessed team ID.
implementation_notes: Last accessed team ID is stored on the user record and updated on team switch. Login flow queries this field and validates team access before redirecting.
comment_summary: Developer implemented on development and deployed for review.
